# 23andMe’s Wojcicki: “We failed to communicate proactively”

By Michal Lev-Ram Michal Lev-Ram

Special Correspondent

By Michal Lev-Ram Michal Lev-Ram

Special Correspondent

December 4, 2013, 12:04 AM ET Add us on

FORTUNE — Less than two weeks ago, the Food and Drug Administration sent a rather [terse letter](http://www.fda.gov/ICECI/EnforcementActions/WarningLetters/2013/ucm376296.htm) to Anne Wojcicki, founder and CEO of Google-backed 23andMe. The jargon-filled message asked Wojcicki [to stop marketing her company’s $99 DNA testing kits because she had failed to receive “FDA marketing authorization for the device.”](http://tech.fortune.cnn.com/2013/11/26/the-curious-case-of-23andme/) In her first media interview since the headline-making letter (and 23andMe’s equally terse [response](http://mediacenter.23andme.com/press-releases/fda-letter-2013/) to the FDA), Wojcicki told _Fortune_ that her company fell behind schedule and failed to communicate proactively with
